faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How much smaller will AVIF files be compared to JPG?
faq.a1: AVIF files are typically 30–50% smaller than JPG at the same visual quality. A 1 MB JPG photo might become 500–700 KB in AVIF with no perceptible quality difference.
faq.q2: Will converting JPG to AVIF reduce image quality?
faq.a2: At recommended quality settings (60–80%), the visual difference is imperceptible to the human eye. AVIF's advanced compression preserves detail better than JPG at equivalent file sizes.
faq.q3: Is it safe to convert my private photos here?
faq.a3: Yes. Avifkit processes everything client-side — your images are converted directly in your browser and are never uploaded to any external server. Your files stay on your device at all times.
faq.a4: Chrome 85+, Firefox 93+, Opera 71+, Edge 121+, and Safari 16.4+ all support AVIF. This covers approximately 93% of global browser usage.
faq.q5: Can I use AVIF images on my website?
faq.a5: Yes! Use the HTML 'picture' element to serve AVIF with a JPG fallback for older browsers, so all visitors get the best format their browser supports.
faq.q6: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
faq.a6: Each file can be up to 10 MB. There are no watermarks, no registration required, and no daily usage caps.
faq.q7: Can I batch convert JPG to AVIF?
faq.a7: Yes! Upload up to 30 JPG files at once. Click Convert All, then download individually or as a ZIP archive.
faq.q8: What quality setting should I use for AVIF?
faq.a8: For web images, 60–75% quality provides excellent results with dramatic size savings. For photography, use 75–85%. For near-lossless quality, use 90%+.
faq.q9: Do I need to create an account?
faq.a9: No. Avifkit is completely free with no registration, no watermarks, and no usage limits.
